title: Inserting Rows in Excel Table with Data Validation
author: Lokesh10Ki
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

After creating an Excel sheet and applying protection and data validations, inserting a row by right-clicking on the row header causes the new row to inherit the properties (data validations and protection) of the row above. It would be beneficial to not apply such validations by default and allow this behavior to be set through options.
6
Lib version: 4.4.0

## Steps To Reproduce

1. Create an Excel sheet using ExcelJS library.
2. Apply data validations and protection to specific rows or cells.
3. Right-click on the row header where you want to insert a new row.
4. Insert a new row using the context menu option.
5. Notice that the new row inherits the data validation and protection settings from the row above.

## The expected behaviour:

When a new row is inserted, it should not inherit the data validation and protection properties of the row above it by default. Instead, these properties should be applied only if explicitly set through the options.

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

Currently, there isn't a built-in option in ExcelJS to prevent inheriting data validations and protection when inserting rows interactively. However, a potential solution could involve:

Providing a method or flag in ExcelJS that allows developers to specify whether new rows should inherit these properties.
Implementing a workaround where validations and protections are applied programmatically after inserting a new row to override inherited settings.
This approach would provide more flexibility and control over how data validations and protections are managed during row insertion in ExcelJS.
